<div class="side-box-flex">
<div class="side-box-image"><span class="skin-invert" typeof="mw:File"><span><img alt="" resource="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Ge'ez.svg" src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1a/Ge%27ez.svg/40px-Ge%27ez.svg.png" decoding="async" data-file-width="232" data-file-height="102" data-file-type="drawing" height="18" width="40" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1a/Ge%27ez.svg/120px-Ge%27ez.svg.png 2x" class="mw-file-element"></span></span></div>
<div class="side-box-text plainlist"><b>This article contains <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ge'ez_script" title="Ge'ez script" class="mw-redirect">Ethiopic text</a>.</b> Without proper <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:Multilingual_support_(Ethiopic)" title="Help:Multilingual support (Ethiopic)">rendering support</a>, you may see <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Specials_(Unicode_block)#Replacement_character" title="Specials (Unicode block)">question marks, boxes, or other symbols</a><span typeof="mw:Entity"> </span>instead of Ethiopic characters.</div></div>
</div>
<p id="mwDA"><b id="mwDQ">Tadbaba Maryam</b>, (<a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ge'ez" title="Ge'ez" class="mw-redirect" id="mwDg">Ge'ez</a>: ተድባበ ማርያም/ጽዮን) also called <b id="mwDw">Tadbaba Zion</b> in ancient times (meaning "Umbrella of St Mary/Zion"), is an <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ethiopian_Orthodox_Tewahedo_Church" title="Ethiopian Orthodox Tewahedo Church" id="mwEA">Ethiopian Orthodox Tewahedo</a> <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Church_(building)" title="Church (building)" id="mwEQ">church</a> located in the place of <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sayint" title="Sayint" id="mwEg">Sayint</a> in <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/South_Wollo" title="South Wollo" class="mw-redirect" id="mwEw">South Wollo</a>, <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amhara_Region" title="Amhara Region" id="mwFA">Amhara Region</a>, <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ethiopia" title="Ethiopia" id="mwFQ">Ethiopia</a>. The current church bears the title in full "Head of Churches and Monasteries Tadbaba Maryam" (also in <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amharic_language" title="Amharic language" about="#mwt21" typeof="mw:Transclusion" class="mw-redirect" id="mwFg" data-mw="{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"langx","href":"./Template:Langx"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"am"},"2":{"wt":"ርዕሰ አድባራት ወገዳማት ተድባበ ማርያም"}},"i":0}}]}">Amharic</a><span about="#mwt21">: </span><span lang="am" about="#mwt21">ርዕሰ አድባራት ወገዳማት ተድባበ ማርያም</span><link rel="mw:PageProp/Category" href="./Category:Articles_containing_Amharic-language_text" about="#mwt21" id="mwFw">).</p>

<meta property="mw:PageProp/toc" id="mwGA" data-mw="{"autoGenerated":true}"></section><section data-mw-section-id="1" id="mwGQ" aria-labelledby="History"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="History">History</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Tadbaba_Maryam&action=edit&section=1" title="Edit section: History"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwGg">Tadbaba Maryam was amongst the first four Temples where sacrificial offerings were implemented in Ethiopia before birth of <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jesus" title="Jesus" id="mwGw">Jesus</a>.  The name is a combination two <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ge'ez" title="Ge'ez" class="mw-redirect" id="mwHA">Ge'ez</a> words: Tadbaba means <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tabernacle" title="Tabernacle" id="mwHQ">Tabernacle</a>, Maryam/ Tsion means <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zion" title="Zion" id="mwHg">Zion</a> which gives 'The Tabernacle of Zion'. According to the chronicle of the Tadbaba Maryam, the Ark of Tadbaba Maryam arrived in 982 BCE which coincides with disappearance of the <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ark_of_the_Covenant" title="Ark of the Covenant" id="mwHw">Ark of the Covenant</a> or the reign of King <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Solomon" title="Solomon" id="mwIA">Solomon</a> (between 970 and 931 BCE).<sup about="#mwt24" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-2" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-2"}}"><a href="#cite_note-2" id="mwIQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwIg"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwIw">[</span>2<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwJA">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt27" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-3" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-3"}}"><a href="#cite_note-3" id="mwJQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwJg"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwJw">[</span>3<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwKA">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt30" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-4" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-4"}}"><a href="#cite_note-4" id="mwKQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwKg"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwKw">[</span>4<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwLA">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt33" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-5" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-5"}}"><a href="#cite_note-5" id="mwLQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwLg"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwLw">[</span>5<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwMA">]</span></span></a></sup></p>

</section><section data-mw-section-id="2" id="mwMQ" aria-labelledby="Construction"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Construction">Construction</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Tadbaba_Maryam&action=edit&section=2" title="Edit section: Construction"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwMg">The current church of Tadbaba Maryam is spectacular in its design and was built by Emperor <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gelawdewos" title="Gelawdewos" id="mwMw">Gelawdewos</a> after he won the war with <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ahmad_ibn_Ibrahim_al-Ghazi" title="Ahmad ibn Ibrahim al-Ghazi" id="mwNA">Ahmad ibn Ibrahim al-Ghazi</a>, the <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Wayna_Daga" title="Battle of Wayna Daga" id="mwNQ">Battle of Wayna Daga</a>. The Ark was carried into the <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Wayna_Daga" title="Battle of Wayna Daga" id="mwNg">Battle of Wayna Daga</a>, the final battle against <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ahmad_ibn_Ibrahim_al-Ghazi" title="Ahmad ibn Ibrahim al-Ghazi" id="mwNw">Ahmad ibn Ibrahim al-Ghazi</a>, where Gelawdewos cut off the Imam's head. Prior to <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gelawdewos" title="Gelawdewos" id="mwOA">Gelawdewos</a>, while kept in the <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tabernacle" title="Tabernacle" id="mwOQ">Tabernacle</a>, the Ark was moved around, but was eventually placed in the Temple founded by King <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gelawdewos" title="Gelawdewos" id="mwOg">Gelawdewos</a>.<sup about="#mwt36" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-6" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-6"}}"><a href="#cite_note-6" id="mwOw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwPA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwPQ">[</span>6<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwPg">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt39" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-7" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-7"}}"><a href="#cite_note-7" id="mwPw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwQA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwQQ">[</span>7<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwQg">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt42" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-8" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-8"}}"><a href="#cite_note-8" id="mwQw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwRA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwRQ">[</span>8<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwRg">]</span></span></a></sup></p>

<p id="mwRw">The church is a circular complex with four doors (>3m height) and 32 windows (>2m height) on the outside. By design, the church is arranged in concentric circles with three sections. The inner most section at the center being the inner sanctum (<a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Holy_of_Holies" title="Holy of Holies" id="mwSA">Holy of Holies</a>) and is about 24 meters in diameter. This is where the <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ark_of_the_Covenant" title="Ark of the Covenant" id="mwSQ">Ark of the Covenant</a> is believed to be kept, explicitly inside a tent (<a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tabernacle" title="Tabernacle" id="mwSg">Tabernacle</a>). In its current form, the church overall measures about 34 meters in diameter.<link rel="mw:PageProp/Category" href="./Category:All_articles_with_unsourced_statements" about="#mwt43" typeof="mw:Transclusion" id="mwSw" data-mw="{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"cn","href":"./Template:Cn"},"params":{"date":{"wt":"August 2021"}},"i":0}}]}"><link rel="mw:PageProp/Category" href="./Category:Articles_with_unsourced_statements_from_August_2021" about="#mwt43"><sup class="noprint Inline-Template Template-Fact" style="white-space:nowrap;" about="#mwt43" id="mwTA"><span typeof="mw:Entity">[</span><i><a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed" title="Wikipedia:Citation needed"><span title="This claim needs references to reliable sources. (August 2021)">citation needed</span></a></i><span typeof="mw:Entity">]</span></sup></p>

</section><section data-mw-section-id="3" id="mwTQ" aria-labelledby="Religious_activity"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Religious_activity">Religious activity</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Tadbaba_Maryam&action=edit&section=3" title="Edit section: Religious activity"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwTg">The high priest are always selected from tribes of the priestly class, believed to be descendants of Jews. In additions, Deacons, only under the age of 9, are allowed to deliver service. Most of the relics and utilities used in the church are made of pure gold including incense burner (censer with chain). When entering the Holy of Holies, a rope is tied to high priest to enable assistants to safely pull body of the high priest out of the inner-sanctum in the event of mishap. <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abuna_Takla_Haymanot" title="Abuna Takla Haymanot" id="mwTw">Abuna Takla Haymanot</a>, 3rd Patriarch of EOC, attempted to enter the Holy of Holies and was pulled out by a rope, but died a year later in 1988.<link rel="mw:PageProp/Category" href="./Category:All_articles_with_unsourced_statements" about="#mwt44" typeof="mw:Transclusion" id="mwUA" data-mw="{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"cn","href":"./Template:Cn"},"params":{"date":{"wt":"August 2021"}},"i":0}}]}"><link rel="mw:PageProp/Category" href="./Category:Articles_with_unsourced_statements_from_August_2021" about="#mwt44"><sup class="noprint Inline-Template Template-Fact" style="white-space:nowrap;" about="#mwt44" id="mwUQ"><span typeof="mw:Entity">[</span><i><a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed" title="Wikipedia:Citation needed"><span title="This claim needs references to reliable sources. (August 2021)">citation needed</span></a></i><span typeof="mw:Entity">]</span></sup></p>

<p id="mwUg">A story is told about a plague that infested the land following an event of mishap in front of the Ark during an annual festivity. Later the people of the land were also afflicted of boils and disease. Since then, the Ark has never been carried outside of its tent or shown to the people during congregation.<link rel="mw:PageProp/Category" href="./Category:All_articles_with_unsourced_statements" about="#mwt45" typeof="mw:Transclusion" id="mwUw" data-mw="{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"cn","href":"./Template:Cn"},"params":{"date":{"wt":"August 2021"}},"i":0}}]}"><link rel="mw:PageProp/Category" href="./Category:Articles_with_unsourced_statements_from_August_2021" about="#mwt45"><sup class="noprint Inline-Template Template-Fact" style="white-space:nowrap;" about="#mwt45" id="mwVA"><span typeof="mw:Entity">[</span><i><a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed" title="Wikipedia:Citation needed"><span title="This claim needs references to reliable sources. (August 2021)">citation needed</span></a></i><span typeof="mw:Entity">]</span></sup></p>

</section><section data-mw-section-id="4" id="mwVQ" aria-labelledby="Treasures_gallery"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Treasures_gallery">Treasures gallery</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Tadbaba_Maryam&action=edit&section=4" title="Edit section: Treasures gallery"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwVg">Tadbaba Maryam possesses a huge collection of treasures, some of them date back to the time of the <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Old_Testament" title="Old Testament" id="mwVw">Old Testament</a>. The collections are now displayed in a museum  inside the churches compound. The most known relics include:</p>

<ul id="mwWA"><li id="mwWQ">An ancient cross with special design on it in the form of a Sheep. Also called Na'wa Bagu, (Behold the Lamb).<sup about="#mwt48" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-Spencer_9-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Spencer"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-Spencer-9"}}"><a href="#cite_note-Spencer-9" id="mwWg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwWw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwXA">[</span>9<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwXQ">]</span></span></a></sup></li>
<li id="mwXg">The Shield of Atse Kaleb used in defeating the rebellious faction in Yemen.</li>
<li id="mwXw">Hebrew Synodus.</li>
<li id="mwYA">Liturgy in Aramaic language.</li>
<li id="mwYQ">Books written in Aramaic language.</li>
<li id="mwYg">Several books written in Ge'ez and Arabic language.</li>
<li id="mwYw">Gospel decorated with Gold.</li>
<li id="mwZA">Hand cross used by the first Ethiopian Patriarch.</li>
<li id="mwZQ">Cross Used by St. John the Baptist.</li>
<li id="mwZg">Hand cross used by Abune Echege Yohannes.<sup about="#mwt49" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-Spencer_9-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Spencer"}}"><a href="#cite_note-Spencer-9" id="mwZw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwaA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwaQ">[</span>9<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwag">]</span></span></a></sup></li>
<li id="mwaw">Hand cross used by Abune Anorios.</li>
<li id="mwbA">Hand cross used by Abune Kerlos.</li>
<li id="mwbQ">A section of the hair of Saint Anne (the mother of St. Mary).</li>
<li id="mwbg">A collection of remains of innocent children massacred by king Herod.</li>
<li id="mwbw">Relics of St. George (the thumb bone).</li>
<li id="mwcA">Relics of other unspecified martyrs (about 28 skeletons).</li>
<li id="mwcQ">Remains of more than 6 kings including Galawdewos.<sup about="#mwt50" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-Spencer_9-2" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Spencer"}}"><a href="#cite_note-Spencer-9" id="mwcg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwcw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwdA">[</span>9<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwdQ">]</span></span></a></sup></li>
<li id="mwdg">Throne of Emperor <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dawit_I" title="Dawit I" id="mwdw">Dawit I</a></li>
<li id="mweA">The Book of Adam.</li>
<li id="mweQ">More than 1000 unspecified manuscripts.</li>
<li id="mweg">St. Luke Ikons of St. Mary. Also called Se'el Adheno (The Saving Picture).<sup about="#mwt51" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-Spencer_9-3" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Spencer"}}"><a href="#cite_note-Spencer-9" id="mwew"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwfA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwfQ">[</span>9<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwfg">]</span></span></a></sup></li>
<li id="mwfw">Several unspecified hand crosses.</li>
<li id="mwgA">Various unspecified ancient relics.</li>
<li id="mwgQ">Gifts provided by various kings and Patriarchs.</li>
<li id="mwgg">A signed collection of the Miracles of Mary, paintings by <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brancaleon" title="Brancaleon" class="mw-redirect" id="mwgw">Brancaleon</a>. <sup about="#mwt54" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-10" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-10"}}"><a href="#cite_note-10" id="mwhA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwhQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwhg">[</span>10<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwhw">]</span></span></a></sup></li></ul>
